name: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 Security and Privacy Controls
description: Selected controls from key families (AC, AU, IA, SC, SI, CM, IR, CP) mapped to Azure implementations. Summaries are paraphrased.

controls.title: Account Management
controls.summary: Accounts must be created, reviewed, disabled, and removed through a managed lifecycle.
controls.remediation: Use Entra ID Governance: entitlement management, access reviews, and automated deprovisioning via HR-driven provisioning.

controls.title: Access Enforcement
controls.summary: The system must enforce approved authorizations on every access attempt.
controls.remediation: Enforce Azure RBAC at narrow scopes, resource firewalls, and app-level authorization checks on every API route.

controls.title: Least Privilege
controls.summary: Users and workloads receive only the access required for their tasks.
controls.remediation: Replace broad roles with task-scoped RBAC, use PIM for eligibility, and periodically review effective permissions.

controls.title: Event Logging
controls.summary: Security-relevant events must be defined and logged across the system.

check_hints.fail_message: No audit log destination exists in the architecture.

controls.remediation: Enable diagnostic settings on all resources to Log Analytics; log authentication, authorization, and data-access events.

controls.title: Audit Record Review and Analysis
controls.summary: Logs must be regularly reviewed and analyzed for indications of inappropriate activity.
controls.remediation: Use Sentinel/Defender analytics rules and scheduled KQL hunts; assign review ownership and cadence.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
controls.summary: Users must be uniquely identified and authenticated before access.
controls.remediation: Integrate all applications with Entra ID; prohibit shared accounts; enforce sign-in risk policies.

controls.title: MFA for Privileged Accounts
controls.remediation: Conditional Access requiring phishing-resistant MFA for all privileged directory and Azure roles.

controls.title: Authenticator Management
controls.summary: Credentials and secrets must be issued, stored, rotated, and revoked securely.
controls.remediation: Store workload secrets in Key Vault with rotation; enforce password protection and ban weak passwords in Entra ID; prefer managed identities to eliminate credentials.

controls.title: Denial-of-Service Protection
controls.summary: The system must limit the effect of DoS attacks.
controls.remediation: Use Front Door/DDoS Protection at the edge, WAF rate rules, and APIM throttling; autoscale critical tiers.

controls.title: Boundary Protection
controls.summary: External and key internal boundaries must be monitored and controlled at managed interfaces.

check_hints.fail_message: No managed boundary control (WAF/Firewall) exists at the network edge.

controls.remediation: Terminate ingress at App Gateway WAF/Front Door, egress via Azure Firewall, deny-by-default NSGs between tiers.

controls.title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity
controls.summary: Transmitted data must be protected from disclosure and modification.
controls.remediation: TLS 1.2+ everywhere, HTTPS-only app settings, secure transfer on storage, private connectivity for data paths.

controls.title: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
controls.summary: Cryptographic keys require managed generation, distribution, storage, and destruction.
controls.remediation: Manage keys in Key Vault (or Managed HSM), enable rotation policies, restrict access via RBAC and network rules.

controls.title: Cryptographic Protection
controls.summary: The system must implement validated cryptography for data protection.
controls.remediation: Rely on platform encryption (FIPS-validated modules); use TDE, SSE, and CMK where mandated.

controls.title: Flaw Remediation
controls.summary: Software flaws must be identified, reported, and corrected within defined timeframes.
controls.remediation: Use Defender vulnerability management, patch SLAs by severity, and CI dependency/container scanning to block known-vulnerable builds.

controls.title: System Monitoring
controls.summary: The system must be monitored to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.
controls.remediation: Enable Defender plans, Sentinel analytics, and application-level anomaly alerts via Application Insights.

controls.title: Baseline Configuration
controls.summary: Current baseline configurations must be developed, documented, and maintained.
controls.remediation: Define baselines as IaC (Bicep) in version control; drift-detect with Azure Policy and what-if deployments.

controls.title: Configuration Settings
controls.summary: Mandatory security settings must be established, enforced, and monitored.
controls.remediation: Assign Azure Policy initiatives (MCSB) with deny/deployIfNotExists effects; scan IaC in CI with Checkov/PSRule.

controls.title: Incident Handling
controls.summary: An incident handling capability covering preparation, detection, containment, eradication, and recovery must exist.
controls.remediation: Maintain Azure-specific IR runbooks, configure Defender security contacts and automation (Logic Apps), rehearse with tabletop exercises.

controls.title: System Backup
controls.summary: Backups of information and system state must be conducted and protected.
controls.remediation: Enable Azure Backup/PITR for databases, immutable storage for backup data, and test restores on a schedule.
